why_them: Shopify e-commerce site already in place with UK-wide mail order and RHS Gold Medal recognition since 2009, but several listed products are marked Sold Out with no restock alerts or customer reviews visible. SEO content is thin for their nationally significant Meconopsis and Scilla National Plant Collections. Open only April to August, so off-season email capture and pre-order marketing represent a clear untapped revenue opportunity.
recommended_service: SEO content strategy and email marketing setup for off-season pre-orders
outreach_hook: Your Shopify store has no restock alerts or reviews — you're likely losing repeat buyers between each April opening.

location: Cally Avenue, Gatehouse of Fleet, Castle Douglas, DG7 2DJ
why_them: The primary domain callygardens.co.uk throws an SSL certificate error in all modern browsers making it completely inaccessible, while the actual e-commerce shop lives on an unbranded Square subdomain. Product reviews are disabled, no phone number is visible on the Square site, and all SEO traffic landing on the main domain bounces on the security warning. Kevin Hughes reopened this renowned walled garden in 2018 with a compelling rare-plants story that is entirely absent from the broken digital presence.
recommended_service: New custom website with working SSL, Shopify or WooCommerce migration, and SEO content
outreach_hook: Your main website gives every visitor a browser security warning — they're leaving before they ever find your plant shop.

location: Carty Port, Newton Stewart, Wigtownshire, DG8 6AY
why_them: Galloway Heathers runs a mail-order heather plant business with an active social presence, but their gallowayheathers.com SSL certificate has expired — serving a security error to every visitor before they can browse or buy. The site runs on a legacy ASP platform with a dated design and no evident mobile responsiveness. A niche specialist nursery with an established mail-order history is losing online sales daily due to a broken storefront that all directory listings still point customers toward.
recommended_service: Emergency SSL fix and new mobile-responsive website with rebuilt online shop
outreach_hook: Right now every customer clicking your website link sees a security warning and cannot place an order — your SSL has expired.

location: Old Minnigaff, Newton Stewart, Wigtownshire, DG8 6PX
why_them: Elmlea Plants has traded since 2000 growing herbaceous perennials and grasses with a mail-order operation expanded during the pandemic, but their website is effectively offline — the www subdomain has an SSL certificate mismatch and the /shop/ URL returns a blank Apache server default page. VisitScotland and Scotland's Garden both direct customers to this broken URL. A family nursery that invested in digital during lockdown has lost its functioning online shop without apparent awareness.
recommended_service: New website build with correct SSL and working WooCommerce or Shopify storefront
outreach_hook: Your online shop is returning a blank server page — customers sent from VisitScotland have nowhere to actually buy your plants.

location: Whinnieliggate, Kirkcudbright, Dumfries and Galloway, DG6 4XP
why_them: Buckland Plants is a respected specialist family nursery known for rare woodland bulbs and perennials including Trillium, Meconopsis and Erythronium, open Thursday to Sunday from March to November. Their domain bucklandplants.co.uk has been hijacked by a content-farming operation publishing completely unrelated articles on gaming and nicotine pouches, actively damaging the business name in search results. Scotland's Garden and GardenVisit still point to this hijacked domain. The business has no working web presence and no findable email, relying entirely on phone and foot traffic.
recommended_service: New domain and website build with mail-order capability and brand recovery SEO
outreach_hook: Your old website domain has been taken over by a spam site — anyone searching Buckland Plants online finds gaming and nicotine articles instead.

why_them: Gowanbrae Nursery has operated since 1992 under Robert Lyle BSc Hort, specialising in alpines, perennials, rhododendrons and conifers on the scenic Solway coast road. The business has no website — their only online presence is a single Scotland's Garden directory listing with a BT internet email. Open Tuesday to Sunday, they are invisible to anyone searching Google for nurseries near Dumfries, and entirely miss tourist traffic on the A710 coastal route.
recommended_service: New website build with Google Business Profile setup and local SEO for the A710 tourist corridor
outreach_hook: Thirty years of specialist horticultural knowledge and you're invisible online — tourists on the A710 can't find you without a website.

location: Penpont, Thornhill, Dumfries and Galloway, DG3 4LZ
why_them: Capenoch Gardens is a small nursery set within the Victorian Capenoch Estate outside Penpont, open year-round except Wednesdays, selling plants, garden furniture and produce. The Gmail contact address and Facebook-only presence indicate minimal digital infrastructure. There is no website, no way for customers to check plant stock before visiting, and no Google Business presence to capture search intent. The historic estate setting offers strong visual content potential that is completely unexploited.
recommended_service: Starter website with Google Business Profile and plant availability updates
outreach_hook: Without a website, visitors can't check your stock or opening hours before driving out to Penpont — you're losing casual footfall.

why_them: Heathhall Garden Centre is a third-generation independent family business trading for over 60 years, now operating under the Barrs Garden Centres brand across five locations with a well-built Shopify site. The Heathhall location page lists no direct email, social media links are present but empty, and there is no local Google Maps embed or location-specific SEO. As a large multi-location group with in-house digital setup, the opportunity for Nith Digital is narrower but Google Ads for the Dumfries location could add measurable value.
recommended_service: Local SEO audit and Google Ads campaign for the Dumfries location
outreach_hook: Your Heathhall page has no email or live social links — local Dumfries customers searching for a garden centre can't contact you directly.
